chapter_title.ar: باب نِكَاحِ أَهْلِ الذِّمَّةِ وَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ يُسْلِمُ بَعْضُهُمْ وَ لَا يُسْلِمُ بَعْضٌ أَوْ يُسْلِمُونَ جَمِيعاً
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – The marriage of the Ahl Al-Zimma, and the Polytheists, some of them become Muslim, and some of them do not become Muslims, or they all become Muslims altogether

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/201)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/201)
text: نَصْرَانِيٍّ تَزَوَّجَ نَصْرَانِيَّةً فَأَسْلَمَتْ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا قَالَ قَدِ انْقَطَعَتْ عِصْمَتُهَا مِنْهُ وَ لَا مَهْرَ لَهَا وَ لَا عِدَّةَ عَلَيْهَا مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Abdul Rahman Bin Al Hajjaj,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> regarding a Christian man who married a Christian woman. So she became a Muslim before he had slept with her. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The hindrance from him on her way (divorce) is removed, and there is no ‘مَهْرَ’ (mahr -dower) for her, nor a waiting period upon her, from him’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from ‘Abd al- Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj who has said the following: “About the case of a man who is a Christian and marries a Christian woman who then becomes a Muslim before his going to bed with her, abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The hindrance from him on her way is removed, she has no mahr (dower) and no waiting period because of him.’”
